name: Axolotl (LLM Fine-Tuning Config Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Axolotl community; OpenAccess AI Collective

auth.notes: Local training; HuggingFace Hub for models/datasets

agent_readiness.notes: YAML-config-driven LLM fine-tuning. Define model, dataset, training params in config.yaml. `axolotl train config.yaml`. Training strategies: SFT, DPO, RLHF, LoRA, QLoRA, full fine-tune. Multi-GPU: DeepSpeed, FSDP, torchrun. Flash Attention 2. Dataset formats: Alpaca, ShareGPT, raw completion, instruction-tuning. HuggingFace datasets. Models: Llama 3, Mistral, Phi, Gemma. Integrates with Unsloth for speed. Modal/vast.ai support. Popular in open-source LLM community. More config-driven than Llama-Factory.

gotchas: YAML config can be complex and underdocumented
Breaking changes between versions are common
Multi-GPU setup requires understanding DeepSpeed configs
Error messages can be cryptic on misconfiguration
best_when: Research fine-tuning needing multi-GPU or advanced training strategies
Custom dataset formats and complex training pipelines
avoid_when: Single GPU consumer fine-tuning (Unsloth simpler and faster)
Teams unfamiliar with distributed training concepts
Simple LoRA fine-tuning (Unsloth or TRL directly simpler)
summary: Axolotl is a YAML-config-driven LLM fine-tuning framework supporting SFT, DPO, LoRA, QLoRA, and full fine-tune. Multi-GPU via DeepSpeed/FSDP. Flash Attention 2. Multiple dataset formats (Alpaca, ShareGPT). `axolotl train config.yaml` workflow. Popular in open-source LLM research community. Best for reproducible multi-GPU fine-tuning experiments. Unsloth is simpler and faster for single-GPU consumer hardware.
